title: Relate Subtraction and Division
essential_question: How is subtraction related to division?
strategies: Subtract the same number again and again until you reach 0.
Count how many times you subtracted вЂ” that's the quotient.

pretest.question: Use repeated subtraction. 12 Г· 4 = ?

feedback.correct: Correct!

hints: Division splits a total into equal groups. Use the inverse multiplication fact.

pretest.question: 15 Г· 3 = ? (repeated subtraction)

feedback.correct: Correct!

hints: Division splits a total into equal groups. Use the inverse multiplication fact.

pretest.question: How many times can you subtract 6 from 30?

feedback.correct: Correct!

hints: Repeated subtraction is division. Total Г· each-step = number of jumps.

pretest.question: 24 Г· 8 = ?

feedback.correct: Correct!

hints: Division splits a total into equal groups. Use the inverse multiplication fact.

pretest.question: A number line starts at 40. You make jumps of 5 back to 0. How many jumps?

feedback.correct: Correct!

hints: Each jump is the divisor. Total Г· jump size = number of jumps.

learn.title: Repeated Subtraction
learn.content: Division can be shown by subtracting the same number until you reach 0. The number of times you subtract is the answer (quotient). Example: 18 Г· 6 в†’ subtract 6 three times to reach 0 в†’ 18 Г· 6 = 3.

learn.title: Step-by-Step Example
learn.content: 18 Г· 6: Start with 18. Subtract 6: 18 в€’ 6 = 12. Subtract 6: 12 в€’ 6 = 6. Subtract 6: 6 в€’ 6 = 0. You subtracted 3 times, so 18 Г· 6 = 3.

learn.title: Number Line Model
learn.content: Start at the total on a number line. Jump back by the divisor. Count the jumps. For 20 Г· 4: start at 20, jump back 4 five times: 20 в†’ 16 в†’ 12 в†’ 8 в†’ 4 в†’ 0. Five jumps в†’ 20 Г· 4 = 5.

learn.title: Number Line: Counting Jumps
learn.content: For 30 Г· 5: start at 30 on a number line. Jump back 5 each time: 30 в†’ 25 в†’ 20 в†’ 15 в†’ 10 в†’ 5 в†’ 0. Count the jumps: 6. So 30 Г· 5 = 6. Each backward jump removes one equal group.

learn.title: Subtraction and Division Are Related
learn.content: Repeated subtraction and division always give the same answer. 24 Г· 8: subtract 8 three times (24в†’16в†’8в†’0) OR use the related fact (8 Г— 3 = 24). Both show 24 Г· 8 = 3. Division is a shortcut for repeated subtraction.

equations: Repeated subtraction: 24 в€’ 8 в€’ 8 в€’ 8 = 0 (3 times)

hints: Subtract 7 from 21 again and again. Count how many times you subtract.
21 в€’ 7 = 14, 14 в€’ 7 = 7, 7 в€’ 7 = 0. That's 3 subtractions, so 21 Г· 7 = 3.

feedback.correct: Correct! 21 в€’ 7 three times = 0, so 21 Г· 7 = 3.

try.question: Use repeated subtraction: 21 Г· 7 = ?

hints: Subtract 9 from 36 again and again until you reach 0. Count each step.

feedback.correct: Right! 36 в€’ 9 four times = 0, so 36 Г· 9 = 4.

try.question: How many times can you subtract 9 from 36?

hints: Subtract 8 from 32 again and again until you reach 0.

feedback.correct: Correct! 32 в€’ 8 four times = 0, so 32 Г· 8 = 4.

try.question: How many times can you subtract 8 from 32?

hints: Subtract 6 from 18 again and again until you reach 0. Count each subtraction.
18 в€’ 6 = 12, 12 в€’ 6 = 6, 6 в€’ 6 = 0. That's 3 subtractions, so the answer is 3.

feedback.correct: Right! 18 в€’ 6 three times = 0, so 18 Г· 6 = 3.

try.question: Use repeated subtraction to find 18 Г· 6.

hints: On the number line: 45 в†’ 36 в†’ 27 в†’ 18 в†’ 9 в†’ 0. Count the jumps.
You subtract 9 five times to reach 0. So 45 Г· 9 = 5.

feedback.correct: Correct! 45 в€’ 9 five times = 0, so 45 Г· 9 = 5 jumps.
feedback.incorrect: Jump back 9 each time: 45в†’36в†’27в†’18в†’9в†’0. Count 5 jumps.

try.question: A number line starts at 45. You jump back 9 each time. How many jumps to reach 0?

Count the subtractions: 4. So 16 Г· 4 = 4.

feedback.correct: Correct! 16 в€’ 4 four times = 0, so 16 Г· 4 = 4.

hints: If you subtract 4 six times, you remove 4 Г— 6 = 24 in total.
The starting number must be 24. Check: 24 в€’ 4 в€’ 4 в€’ 4 в€’ 4 в€’ 4 в€’ 4 = 0.

feedback.incorrect: 6 subtractions of 4 = 4 Г— 6 = 24. The starting number is 24.
